taxonomy: Paepalanthus niveus is classified as a species of flowering plant in the family Eriocaulaceae. It belongs to the genus Paepalanthus, which is a large and diverse group of plants. Paepalanthus niveus is native to South America and is often found in grasslands and open areas. The plant is known for its white, cotton-like inflorescences, which give it its name. In the scientific classification, Paepalanthus niveus is further categorized as a member of the subfamily Paepalanthoideae.

intro: Paepalanthus niveus, also known as the white beard grass, is a type of flowering plant that belongs to the family Eriocaulaceae. The plant has been traditionally used in folk medicine to treat various health conditions, including inflammation and respiratory issues, due to its anti-inflammatory and expectorant properties. The bioactive constituents of Paepalanthus niveus include flavonoids, phenolic acids, and alkaloids, which are responsible for its medicinal properties. Herbal preparations made from Paepalanthus niveus include infusions, decoctions, and tinctures, which are often used to treat coughs, colds, and other respiratory ailments.
